mission.title: Install and Configure Langflow
mission.description: Langflow is a powerful platform for building and deploying AI-powered agents and workflows, providing a visual authoring experience and built-in API servers. It is ideal for developers looking to integrate AI workflows into applications without managing complex dependencies. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential resource overhead.

steps.title: Install the Langflow CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Langflow CLI tool which is necessary for managing the Langflow deployment.

```bash

pip install langflow==1.8.0

```

> Ensure Python 3.10–3.13 is installed.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create langflow-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials langflow-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Deploy Langflow
steps.description:
This command deploys Langflow to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add langflow https://charts.langflow.org

helm install langflow langflow/langflow --version 1.8.0 --namespace langflow --create-namespace

```

> Add the Langflow Helm repository.
> Deploy Langflow in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures monitoring and autoscaling for Langflow.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langflow-ai/langflow/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

kubectl autoscale deployment langflow --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10

```

> Set up monitoring with Prometheus.
> Enable autoscaling based on CPU usage.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Langflow
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Langflow deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data stored in the cluster.

```bash

helm uninstall langflow --namespace langflow

gcloud container clusters delete langflow-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This will remove the Langflow deployment.
> This will delete the entire cluster and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Langflow
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This process includes draining nodes if necessary, upgrading the deployment, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n langflow

helm upgrade langflow langflow/langflow --version 1.8.0 --namespace langflow

kubectl get pods -n langflow

```

> Check the status of your pods before upgrading.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the server: dial tcp 35.185.XXX.XXX:443: i/o timeout'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates a network connectivity issue to the Kubernetes API server.

**Fix:**
Check your internet connection and ensure that the cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image 'langflow:1.8.0': Error response from daemon: pull access denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified image does not exist or is not accessible.

**Fix:**
Ensure the image name and tag are correct and that you have access to the repository.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff' on Langflow pods
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Langflow application is failing to start.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n langflow' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the Langflow pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or the resources allocated to the existing nodes.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved the visual builder interface.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with all pods in 'Running' state when everything is working.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
